title: Advanced Analysis
subtitle: Deep insights into your training performance
noAnalysisDataHint: Start logging activities with power data to see your performance analysis.
rampRateWarning: Load is rising quickly – pay attention to recovery.
balanceOvertraining: Overtraining risk. Consider rest or light training.
balanceOptimal: Optimal training zone – you're building fitness.
balanceTransition: Transition zone. Your body is adapting to load.
balanceFresh: Optimal freshness for intense sessions or racing.
nextStepsBase: Focus on building base fitness with consistent training volume.
nextStepsGood: Good fitness base. Add intensity workouts to improve performance.
nextStepsExcellent: Excellent fitness. Balance high-intensity work with adequate recovery.
outlookPeak: Peak form – good time for a race or hard effort.
outlookRecovery: Recovery needed. Focus on light aerobic training.
ctlDescription: Chronic Training Load represents your long-term fitness (42-day average). Higher CTL means better endurance capacity.
atlDescription: Acute Training Load measures recent training stress (7-day average). High ATL indicates you need recovery.
tsbDescription: Training Stress Balance (CTL - ATL) indicates readiness. Optimal training zone: TSB -10 to -35 (building fitness).
